sentences.text: Control-flow Integrity (CFI) capabilities help defend against Return-Oriented Programming (ROP) and Call/Jump-Oriented Programming (COP/JOP) style control-flow subversion attacks.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI)

sentences.text: The Zicfiss and Zicfilp extensions provide backward-edge and forward-edge control flow integrity respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI)

sentences.text: Please see the Control-flow Integrity chapter of the Unprivileged ISA specification for further details on these CFI capabilities and the associated Unprivileged ISA.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI)

sentences.text: This section specifies the Privileged ISA for the Zicfilp extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp)

sentences.text: The term `xLPE` is used to determine if forward-edge CFI using landing pads provided by the Zicfilp extension is enabled at a privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State

sentences.text: When S-mode is implemented, it is determined as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State

sentences.text: .`xLPE` determination when S-mode is implemented [width=100%] | |Privilege Mode| xLPE | M | `mseccfg.MLPE` | S or HS | `menvcfg.LPE` | VS | `henvcfg.LPE` | U or VU | `senvcfg.LPE` |
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State

sentences.text: When S-mode is not implemented, it is determined as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State

sentences.text: .`xLPE` determination when S-mode is not implemented [width=100%] | |Privilege Mode| xLPE | M | `mseccfg.MLPE` | U | `menvcfg.LPE` |
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State

sentences.text: A trap may need to be delivered to the same or to a higher privilege mode upon completion of `JALR`/`C.JALR`/`C.JR`, but before the instruction at the target of indirect call/jump was decoded, due to:
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: Asynchronous interrupts. * Synchronous exceptions with priority higher than that of a software-check exception with `xtval` set to "landing pad fault (code=2)" (See of Privileged Specification).
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: The software-check exception caused by Zicfilp has higher priority than an illegal-instruction exception but lower priority than instruction access-fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: The software-check exception due to the instruction not being an `LPAD` instruction when `ELP` is `LPEXPECTED` or a software-check exception caused by the `LPAD` instruction itself leads to a trap being delivered to the same or to a higher privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: In such cases, the `ELP` prior to the trap, the previous `ELP`, must be preserved by the trap delivery such that it can be restored on a return from the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: To store the previous `ELP` state on trap delivery to M-mode, an `MPELP` bit is provided in the `mstatus` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: To store the previous `ELP` state on trap delivery to S/HS-mode, an `SPELP` bit is provided in the `mstatus` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: The `SPELP` bit in `mstatus` can be accessed through the `sstatus` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: To store the previous `ELP` state on traps to VS-mode, a `SPELP` bit is defined in the `vsstatus` (VS-modes version of `sstatus`).
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: To store the previous `ELP` state on transition to Debug Mode, a `pelp` bit is defined in the `dcsr` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into privilege mode `x`, the `xPELP` is set to `ELP` and `ELP` is set to `NOLPEXPECTED`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: An `MRET` or `SRET` instruction is used to return from a trap in M-mode or S-mode, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: When executing an `xRET` instruction, if the new privilege mode is `y`, then `ELP` is set to the value of `xPELP` if `yLPE` (see ) is 1; otherwise, it is set to `NOLPEXPECTED`; `xPELP` is set to `NOLPEXPECTED`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: Upon entry into Debug Mode, the `pelp` bit in `dcsr` is updated with the `ELP` at the privilege level the hart was previously in, and the `ELP` is set to `NOLPEXPECTED`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: When a hart resumes from Debug Mode, if the new privilege mode is `y`, then `ELP` is set to the value of `pelp` if `yLPE` (see ) is 1; otherwise, it is set to `NOLPEXPECTED`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: See also for semantics added to the RNMI trap and the MNRET instruction when this extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Landing-Pad-Enabled (LPE) State > Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps

sentences.text: This section specifies the Privileged ISA for the Zicfiss extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss)

sentences.text: Attempts to access the `ssp` CSR may result in either an illegal-instruction exception or a virtual-instruction exception, contingent upon the state of the *x*`envcfg.SSE` fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: The conditions are specified as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: If the privilege mode is less than M and `menvcfg.SSE` is 0, an illegal-instruction exception is raised. *
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: Otherwise, if in U-mode and `senvcfg.SSE` is 0, an illegal-instruction exception is raised. *
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: Otherwise, if in VS-mode and `henvcfg.SSE` is 0, a virtual-instruction exception is raised. *
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: Otherwise, if in VU-mode and either `henvcfg.SSE` or `senvcfg.SSE` is 0, a virtual-instruction exception is raised. *
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: Otherwise, the access is allowed.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Pointer (`ssp`) CSR access control

sentences.text: The term `xSSE` is used to determine if backward-edge CFI using shadow stacks provided by the Zicfiss extension is enabled at a privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow-Stack-Enabled (SSE) State

sentences.text: When S-mode is implemented, it is determined as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow-Stack-Enabled (SSE) State

sentences.text: .`xSSE` determination when S-mode is implemented [width=100%] | |Privilege Mode| `xSSE` | M | `0` | S or HS | `menvcfg.SSE` | VS | `henvcfg.SSE` | U or VU | `senvcfg.SSE` |
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow-Stack-Enabled (SSE) State

sentences.text: When S-mode is not implemented, then `xSSE` is 0 at both M and U privilege modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow-Stack-Enabled (SSE) State

sentences.text: NOTE: Changes to `xSSE` take effect immediately; address-translation caches need not be synchronized with SFENCE.VMA, HFENCE.GVMA, or HFENCE.VVMA instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow-Stack-Enabled (SSE) State

sentences.text: To protect shadow stack memory, the memory is associated with a new page type – the Shadow Stack (SS) page – in the single-stage and VS-stage page tables.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The encoding `R=0`, `W=1`, and `X=0`, is defined to represent an SS page.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: When `menvcfg.SSE=0`, this encoding remains reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Similarly, when `V=1` and `henvcfg.SSE=0`, this encoding remains reserved at `VS` and `VU` levels.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: If `satp.MODE` (or `vsatp.MODE` when `V=1`) is set to `Bare` and the effective privilege mode is less than M, shadow stack instructions raise a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: When the effective privilege mode is M, memory access by an `SSAMOSWAP.W/D` instruction results in a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Memory mapped as an SS page cannot be written to by instructions other than `SSAMOSWAP.W/D`, `SSPUSH`, and `C.SSPUSH`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Attempts will raise a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Access to a SS page using cache-block operation (`CBO.*`) instructions is not permitted.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Such accesses will raise a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Implicit accesses, including instruction fetches to an SS page, are not permitted.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Such accesses will raise an access-fault exception appropriate to the access type.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: However, the shadow stack is readable by all instructions that only load from memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: If a shadow stack (SS) instruction raises an access-fault, page-fault, or guest-page-fault exception that is supposed to indicate the original instruction type (load or store/AMO), then the reported exception cause is respectively a store/AMO access fault (code 7), a store/AMO page fault (code 15), or a store/AMO guest-page fault (code 23).
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: For shadow stack instructions, the reported instruction type is always as though it were a store or AMO, even for instructions `SSPOPCHK` and `C.SSPOPCHK` that only read from memory and do not write to it.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Shadow stack instructions are restricted to accessing shadow stack (`pte.xwr=010b`) pages.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Should a shadow stack instruction access a page that is not designated as a shadow stack page and is not marked as read-only (`pte.xwr=001`), a store/AMO access-fault exception will be invoked.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Conversely, if the page being accessed by a shadow stack instruction is a read-only page, a store/AMO page-fault exception will be triggered.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: If the virtual address in `ssp` is not `XLEN` aligned, then the `SSPUSH`/ `C.SSPUSH`/`SSPOPCHK`/`C.SSPOPCHK` instructions cause a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Correct execution of shadow stack instructions that access memory requires the the accessed memory to be idempotent.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: If the memory referenced by `SSPUSH`/`C.SSPUSH`/`SSPOPCHK`/`C.SSPOPCHK`/`SSAMOSWAP.W/D` instructions is not idempotent, then the instructions cause a store/AMO access-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The `U` and `SUM` bit enforcement is performed normally for shadow stack instruction initiated memory accesses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The state of the `MXR` bit does not affect read access to a shadow stack page as the shadow stack page is always readable by all instructions that load from memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The G-stage address translation and protections remain unaffected by the Zicfiss extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The `xwr 010b` encoding in the G-stage PTE remains reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: When G-stage page tables are active, the shadow stack instructions that access memory require the G-stage page table to have read-write permission for the accessed memory; else a store/AMO guest-page-fault exception is raised.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: Svpbmt and Svnapot extensions are supported for shadow stack pages.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The PMA checks are extended to require memory referenced by shadow stack instructions to be idempotent.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The PMP checks are extended to require read-write permission for memory accessed by shadow stack instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: If the PMP does not provide read-write permissions or if the accessed memory is not idempotent then a store/AMO access-fault exception is raised.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection

sentences.text: The `SSAMOSWAP.W/D` instructions require the PMA of the accessed memory range to provide AMOSwap level support.
sentences.section: Preamble > Control-flow Integrity (CFI) > Landing Pad (Zicfilp) > Shadow Stack (Zicfiss) > Shadow Stack Memory Protection
